arts.ttl: Amorim: Saints to teach me more than Arsenal
arts.sum: Ruben Amorim said he will learn more about his players when they face Southampton than during the performances against Liverpool and Arsenal.

arts.ttl: £58.9m player now agrees new contract just days after Arsenal were about to bid - Fabrizio Romano
Two names could become urgent Arsenal transfer priorities before the end of the month
arts.sum: Arsenal could reportedly turn to Benjamin Sesko and Dusan Vlahovic as they urgently look for a new striker in this January’s transfer window. The Gunners have just lost Gabriel Jesus to a serious injury, and manager Mikel Arteta will surely be aware that reinforcements are needed if his team are to stay in the title […] The post Two names could become urgent Arsenal transfer priorities before the end of the month appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: ‘Arteta wants him’: Arsenal can afford their top transfer target, but there’s a problem – journalist
arts.sum: Arsenal need to have a busy January transfer window. Arsenal are just lagging behind a little bit in the Premier League title race at the...

arts.ttl: Havertz reaction, Arteta plan, injury impact - 3 things spotted in Arsenal training for Tottenham - football.london
arts.sum: For Arsenal, the battle is that a defeat here to their arch-rivals would almost certainly put a stopper on their ambitions to overthrow Liverpool, or at least that is how it feels given the clunky nature of recent performances. Arteta addressed the issue well on Tuesday and Havertz appears to be getting on with things well despite the toll this must have.

arts.ttl: Stan Kroenke could sanction Arsenal striker signing as £100m loan opportunity emerges
arts.sum: football.london reported last year that Arsenal had been tracking Ferguson, with the striker believed to be a player of interest. “We will negotiate what is important for him, what is important for his development, where he can improve the best and then we will make the decision together,” Hurzeler said on Ferguson’s future ahead of Brighton’s Premier League trip to Ipswich on Thursday.

arts.ttl: Tottenham hope, Arsenal set-piece concern, Son and Gabriel goals - North London Derby predicted - Lee Wilmot - football.london
arts.sum: An Arsenal fan friend of mine is extremely pessimistic about tonight's game, going as far as to say 'I think Tottenham will win.' It's in my nature, as a Spurs fan, to be pessimistic myself. Given it's a North London Derby, you'd expect good Tottenham to be up for it.

arts.ttl: Martin Zubimendi to Arsenal: Fabrizio Romano reveals state of play
arts.sum: Fabrizio Romano has confirmed that Arsenal are in advanced talks to sign Martin Zubimendi as he reveals the state of play of the Real Sociedad midfielder’s move to the Emirates. It is claimed that sources close to the negotiation believe that a deal is 'virtually completed.' Naturally, Arsenal want the 25-year-old to join immediately, but Real Sociedad are insisting that he remains with them until the end of the season.
